name: eurelis-llmatoolkit

metadata.name: eurelis-llmatoolkit
metadata.summary: Framework to build and manage IA based applications. Based on Langchain under the hood.

documentation:
# Eurelis-LlmaToolkit

![Python : 11](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-=3.11-green)
![Code style : black](https://img.shields.io/badge/Code_style-black-black)
![Linting : pylint](https://img.shields.io/badge/Linting-pylint-yellowgreen)

Framework to build and manage IA based applications. Based on Langchain under the hood.
Reboot de la librairie [Eurelis-KB-Framework](https://github.com/Eurelis/Eurelis-KB-Framework).

classes.name: eurelis_llmatoolkit.langchain.vectorstores.mongodb.mongodb_similarity_atlas_vector_store_search.MongoDBSimilarityAtlasVectorStoreSearch

methods.name: eurelis_llmatoolkit.langchain.vectorstores.mongodb.mongodb_similarity_atlas_vector_store_search.MongoDBSimilarityAtlasVectorStoreSearch._select_relevance_score_fn
documentation:
The 'correct' relevance function
may differ depending on a few things, including:
- the distance / similarity metric used by the VectorStore
- the scale of your embeddings (OpenAI's are unit normed. Many others are not!)
- embedding dimensionality
- etc.
Vectorstores should define their own selection based method of relevance.

methods.name: eurelis_llmatoolkit.langchain.vectorstores.mongodb.mongodb_similarity_atlas_vector_store_search.MongoDBSimilarityAtlasVectorStoreSearch.add_documents

Args:
    documents (List[Document]: Documents to add to the vectorstore.

Returns:

methods.name: eurelis_llmatoolkit.langchain.vectorstores.mongodb.mongodb_similarity_atlas_vector_store_search.MongoDBSimilarityAtlasVectorStoreSearch.delete
documentation:
Delete by vector ID or other criteria.

Args:
    ids: List of ids to delete.
    **kwargs: Other keyword arguments that subclasses might use.

Returns:
    Optional[bool]: True if deletion is successful,
    False otherwise, None if not implemented.
Class to enable similarity search with score on mongodb

methods.name: eurelis_llmatoolkit.langchain.langchain_wrapper.LangchainWrapper.mean_embedding_from_metadata_search_documents
documentation:
Method to fetch k document using filters vector store and compute a mean embedding
Args:
    k: max number of documents to return
    search_filter: filter
    mean_embedding_method: you can pass a method taking an Embeddings object and a sequence of documents
        and returning a vector

Returns:
    embedding, list of float

methods.name: eurelis_llmatoolkit.langchain.langchain_wrapper.LangchainWrapper.get_embedding_associated_documents
documentation:
Method to get suggestions

Args:
    *sources(str): list of sources
    source_field(str): the metadata field to use to filter sources for
    k(int): the expected number of documents to return
    expected_docs_by_source(int): the expected number of documents for a given source
    single_doc_by_source(bool): default to True, only one document for a given source in the result list
    source_mean_embedding_method: to specify a callable to compute mean embedding for a document sequence

Returns:
    list of tuples with the document and relevance score

methods.name: eurelis_llmatoolkit.langchain.langchain_wrapper.LangchainWrapper.vector_search_documents
documentation:
Method to execute a similarity search on the vector store
Args:
    vector: the query to look documents for
    k: max number of documents to return
    search_filter: filter
    include_relevance: boolean, default

Returns:
    list of documents

methods.name: eurelis_llmatoolkit.langchain.langchain_wrapper.LangchainWrapper.search_documents
documentation:
Method to execute a similarity search on the vector store
Args:
    query (str): the query to look documents for
    k (int): max number of documents to return
    search_filter: filter
    for_print (bool): default to False, if True method will always print result in the console
    include_relevance (bool): should we include relevance in results

Returns:
    list of documents

functions.name: eurelis_llmatoolkit.langchain.document_transformers.urloutput._strip_scheme
documentation:
Function to remove the scheme from an url

Parse an url then return it removing its scheme and leading "/" characcters
ie: https://apple.com => //apple.com => apple.com

Args:
    url (str): an url as a string

Returns:
    str: the url without its scheme

methods.name: eurelis_llmatoolkit.langchain.document_transformers.urloutput.UrlOutputTransformer.transform_documents
documentation:
Add a new metadata field containing a relative path given a source url.

This method will extract the url from the document metadata, then remove its scheme and add it back to metadata
in a new field.

Args:
    documents: A sequence of documents to be transformed.
    **kwargs: Arbitrary keyword arguments.

Yields:
    Sequence[Document]: sequence of transformed documents

methods.name: eurelis_llmatoolkit.langchain.document_transformers.urloutput.UrlOutputTransformer.atransform_documents
documentation:
Asynchronously add a new metadata field containing a relative path given a source url.

This method isn't implemented

Raises:
    NotImplementedError

classes.name: eurelis_llmatoolkit.langchain.document_transformers.urloutput.UrlOutputTransformerFactory

methods.name: eurelis_llmatoolkit.langchain.document_transformers.urloutput.UrlOutputTransformerFactory.build
documentation:
Construct the UrlOutputTransformer
Args:
    context: the context object, usually the current langchain wrapper instance

Returns:
    a UrlOutputTransformer instance

Factory for the UrlOutputTransformer
It is used to convert a metadata source value containing an url, to a source_output value containing a relative path
Example,
source = https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html
=> source_output = docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html

It is mostly used for caching

documentation:
Asynchronously transform a list of documents.

Args:
    documents: A sequence of Documents to be transformed.

Returns:
    A list of transformed Documents.

Based on langchain BaseIteratorTransformer, but using iterable instead of sequence

Abstract base class for document transformation systems.

A document transformation system takes an iterable of Documents and returns an iterable of transformed Documents.

methods.name: eurelis_llmatoolkit.utils.class_loader.ClassLoader._extract_class_name_data
documentation:
Static method to extract the module name and class name from a qualified class name

Args:
    qualified_class_name (str): Qualified class name.
    default_module_name (str): Default module to use in the case the given classname isn't qualified

Returns:
    Tuple[str, str]: (module_name, class_name) tuple extracted

methods.name: eurelis_llmatoolkit.utils.class_loader.ClassLoader._load_module
documentation:
Load a module from its name, cache the result

Args:
    module_name (str): import path of a module to load

Returns:
    ModuleType: module object

methods.name: eurelis_llmatoolkit.utils.class_loader.ClassLoader.instantiate_class
documentation:
Instantiate a class.

Args:
    default_module_name (str): default module name for the class if a fully qualified name is not given
    class_data (str|dict): either a string (name or fully qualified name of the class) either a dictionary to construct the class from

Returns:
    object: a class instance

Raises:
    ValueError: Raises an error if class_data is neither a string nor a dictionary.

methods.name: eurelis_llmatoolkit.utils.class_loader.ClassLoader._instantiate_class
documentation:
Instantiate a class with its name and arguments.

Args:
    default_module_name (str): the default module name to use if the class name isn't qualified
    qualified_class_name (str): the class name, can be qualified or not
    *args: positional arguments to provide the class for instantiation
    **kwargs: keyword arguments to provide the class for instantiation

Returns:
    object: a class instance

methods.name: eurelis_llmatoolkit.utils.class_loader.ClassLoader._instantiate_class_from_dict
documentation:
Instantiate a class from an object representation

Args:
    default_module_name: the default module name of the class
    class_dict: object representation of the class, must contain a value for the 'class' key,
        can contain values for 'args' and 'kwargs' values

Returns:
    object: a class instance

Raises:
    ValueError: Raises an error if 'class' attribute is missing from the instance definition.

methods.name: eurelis_llmatoolkit.utils.class_loader.ClassLoader.instantiate_factory
documentation:
Instantiate a factory object.

This method will instantiate a factory object given default module and class values and additional data either
as a string (to give the class to use) or a dictionary (to give parameters to the factory instance, can also
give the class to use)

Args:
    default_module_name (str): The default module name for the factory class.
    default_factory_class (str): The default class name for the factory class.
    factory_data (str|dict): either a string (factory class name or fully qualified factory class name) or a
        dictionary to construct the factory

Returns:
    BaseFactory: a factory object

methods.name: eurelis_llmatoolkit.utils.class_loader.ClassLoader._instantiate_factory
documentation:
Instantiate a factory with its default module, its name and its arguments

Args:
    default_module_name (str): the default module name to use if the factory class name isn't qualified
    qualified_class_name (str): the factory class name, can be qualified or not

Returns:
    BaseFactory: a factory instance

methods.name: eurelis_llmatoolkit.utils.class_loader.ClassLoader._instantiate_factory_from_dict
documentation:
Instantiate a factory with its module, its name and an object representation

Args:
    default_module_name: the default module name to use if the factory class name isn't qualified
    default_factory_class: the factory class name, can be qualified or not
    factory_dict: object representation of the factory class, can contain a value for the 'factory' key
        (to override the default factory class), can contain values for 'args' and 'kwargs' values

Returns:
    a factory instance
To dynamically instantiate classes by either their module and name or fully qualified name
